# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

FROM ubuntu:22.04

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

# Create a non-root user for running the application
RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser -m -s /bin/bash appuser

# Update package lists
RUN apt-get update

# Install Node.js prerequisites
RUN apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends ca-certificates=20230311ubuntu0.22.04.1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends curl=7.81.0-1ubuntu1.18 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends gnupg=2.2.27-3ubuntu2.1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Add NodeSource repository for Node.js 20.x LTS
RUN curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash -

# Install Node.js
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends nodejs=20.* \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install build tools
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends build-essential=12.9ubuntu3 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends python3=3.10.6-1~22.04 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install utility packages
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends git=1:2.34.1-1ubuntu1.11 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends wget=1.21.2-2ubuntu1.1 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends jq=1.6-2.1ubuntu3 \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Remove curl and wget after setup is complete to reduce attack surface
# (comment out these lines if they are needed at runtime)
RUN apt-get update && apt-get purge -y curl wget && apt-get autoremove -y \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Set up application directory with proper ownership
RUN mkdir -p /app && chown appuser:appuser /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package files first for better layer caching
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser package*.json ./

# Switch to non-root user before installing dependencies
USER appuser

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm ci --only=production && npm cache clean --force

# Copy application source with proper ownership
COPY --chown=appuser:appuser . .

# Expose application port
EXPOSE 3000

# Use dumb-init or node directly (not npm) to handle signals properly
CMD ["node", "server.js"]
